task_completion.description: Juris-M application is open and active on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Juris-M main window is visible, showing the three-pane layout (library on left, item list in middle, metadata on right).

task_completion.description: A new collection is created within the Juris-M library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new folder/collection icon is visible in the left pane under 'My Library'.

task_completion.description: The newly created collection has a name related to legal content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text label of the new collection in the left pane contains legal-related terminology (e.g., 'Law', 'Cases', 'Supreme Court', 'Legal References').

task_completion.description: Items have been added to the newly created collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The new legal collection is highlighted/selected in the left pane, and the middle pane displays a list of populated items rather than being empty.

task_completion.description: The collection contains at least three legal references (US Supreme Court cases or law review articles).
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the new collection selected, the middle pane lists at least three distinct rows of items. The titles or item types should indicate they are court cases (e.g., 'v.', 'U.S.') or law review articles.

integrity.description: The agent used the Juris-M graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking buttons, using context menus, or dragging and dropping items within Juris-M, rather than using a terminal to execute SQLite database commands.

integrity.description: The agent used existing library items rather than creating fake/dummy items.
integrity.visual_evidence: The items in the collection show rich, pre-existing metadata (creators, dates, publication titles) in the middle or right panes, rather than being newly typed, sparse dummy entries.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No code editors (like VS Code) or terminal windows are shown running Python or bash scripts to modify the Juris-M database directly.
